title: Fix all content fetch hooks — Poetry, Dua, and Songs failing to load

requirements.text: Audit the backend Motoko actor in backend/main.mo and verify that getAllPoetry, getAllDua, and getAllSongs (or their equivalent query functions) have correct function signatures, return types, and can be called without trapping or rejecting. Ensure each function returns an array of the correct record type with all expected fields (id, title, content, category, likeCount, and any media blob fields).

quotes: Showing failed to load content please try again later
auditing and correcting all three fetch hooks (useGetAllPoetry, useGetAllDua, useGetAllSongs) in useQueries.ts
Verify the backend Motoko query functions return the correct types

acceptanceCriteria: getAllPoetry, getAllDua, and getAllSongs backend query functions return valid arrays without trapping
Each function's return type matches the data shape expected by the frontend
Functions are callable by anonymous principals without authorization errors
Empty arrays are returned gracefully when no content has been uploaded yet

requirements.text: Fix the useGetAllPoetry, useGetAllDua, and useGetAllSongs React Query hooks in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts. Ensure each hook calls the correct backend actor method name exactly as defined in the Motoko actor, uses a stable query key, and correctly transforms the backend response (mapping Motoko record field names to frontend TypeScript type field names). Add graceful error handling so a failed fetch shows a user-friendly error state rather than crashing the UI.

quotes: Showing failed to load content please try again later
auditing and correcting all three fetch hooks (useGetAllPoetry, useGetAllDua, useGetAllSongs) in useQueries.ts
Ensuring the frontend data transformation maps backend fields to frontend types correctly

acceptanceCriteria: useGetAllPoetry calls the correct Motoko function name and returns Poetry array without error
useGetAllDua calls the correct Motoko function name and returns Dua array without error
useGetAllSongs calls the correct Motoko function name and returns Song array without error
All field names in the data transformation match the actual Motoko record fields (e.g., likeCount vs like_count, etc.)
When the backend returns an empty array, the UI shows an empty state, not an error
When the backend call genuinely fails, the UI shows a user-friendly error message with a retry option

requirements.text: Verify and fix the PoetryPage, DuaPage, and SongsPage components to ensure their loading skeleton states, error states, and empty states are triggered correctly based on the actual data shapes returned by the fixed hooks. Remove any code that incorrectly maps field names or makes incorrect type assumptions that could cause a valid response to be treated as an error.

quotes: Showing failed to load content please try again later
ensure the error boundary and loading skeleton states work correctly, and that the error alert is not triggered due to incorrect data shape mapping

acceptanceCriteria: PoetryPage, DuaPage, and SongsPage display loading skeletons while data is being fetched
Content renders correctly once data is returned from the backend
Error state is only shown when the backend call actually fails, not due to a data shape mismatch
Empty state is shown when the backend returns a valid empty array
MixPage also renders all three content types correctly after the fetch fixes are applied

constraints: Do not change the PIN authentication logic (PIN: 09186114) or admin route structure
Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
All backend changes must remain within the single-actor architecture in backend/main.mo
nonGoals: Adding new content features or UI sections beyond fixing the broken fetch hooks
Changing the visual design or layout of any page
Altering the Web Audio filter implementation
Changing the hamburger menu or its sections
